synthesis_text: COVID-19 is a contagious disease caused by the coronavirus SARS-CoV-2, which began spreading worldwide in January 2020, leading to a pandemic. The World Health Organization declared it a global health emergency in March 2020 and ended the emergency status in May 2023. The disease can be diagnosed using RT-PCR or other nucleic acid tests, and confirmed with chest CT scans. Preventive measures include vaccination, physical distancing, mask-wearing, and handwashing [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00000, doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00079, node_c:4:f813ab80].

COVID-19 is an illness caused by the virus SARS-CoV-2, which began spreading in late 2019 and became a pandemic in 2020. It spreads mainly through the air via tiny droplets from coughs, sneezes, or talking, especially in close contact. COVID-19 can cause mild illness or no symptoms, but can lead to hospitalization or death in older adults and those with certain conditions. It's possible to get COVID-19 more than once due to fading immunity or virus mutations. Preventive measures include vaccination, good airflow, physical distancing, mask-wearing, and hygiene [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00000, doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001, doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00009, doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00022].

COVID-19 is an infectious respiratory illness caused by the coronavirus SARS-CoV-2. Symptoms can appear 2 to 14 days after exposure and range from mild to severe, with urgent symptoms including difficulty breathing and chest pain. COVID-19 vaccines undergo rigorous testing to ensure safety and effectiveness. Preventive measures include vaccination, physical distancing, mask-wearing, and handwashing [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00000, chunk_00002, chunk_00006, chunk_00012].
